evals.description: ProductCatalog status requirement for configurator visibility
evals.prompt: My products are set up correctly with categories and pricebook entries, but they don't appear in the product configurator when I try to add them to a transaction. What should I check first?
evals.notes: Response must identify ProductCatalog.Status = Active as the first check, not assume a permission or pricebook issue.

evals.description: Product2 + ProductClassification relationship for attribute configurability
evals.prompt: I created a Product2 record with several AttributeDefinition records but the configurator shows no attribute inputs for the product. What is the missing link?
evals.notes: Must identify that Product2.ProductClassificationId is not set, and that ProductClassificationAttribute junctions are required to link attributes to the classification.

evals.description: TransactionLineItem vs OrderItem — correct object for new-core Revenue Cloud
evals.prompt: I need to query all line items on a customer's revenue cloud order. Which object should I query?
evals.notes: Must direct to TransactionLineItem, not OrderItem. OrderItem belongs to the legacy OMS.

evals.description: PriceAdjustmentSchedule AdjustmentType picklist values and value semantics
evals.prompt: I set up a PriceAdjustmentSchedule with AdjustmentValue = 15. Sometimes customers get 15% off and sometimes $15 off. What controls which behavior applies?
evals.notes: Must explain that AdjustmentType determines interpretation of AdjustmentValue. Must name the picklist values PercentageDiscount and FixedDiscount explicitly.

evals.description: BillingSchedule auto-generation — do not create manually
evals.prompt: After activating a transaction with a monthly subscription product, should I create the BillingSchedule records myself or does the platform handle that?
evals.notes: Must state that BillingSchedule records are auto-generated on transaction activation. Must warn against manual creation.

evals.description: Bundle parent-child via ParentTransactionLineItemId — totals from children
evals.prompt: I have a bundle transaction with a parent product and three child components. When I read the parent TransactionLineItem.TotalAmount it shows $0 but the children have prices. Is this a bug?
evals.notes: Must explain that container-only bundle parents are $0 by design and that bundle totals must be summed from child TLIs.

evals.description: AttributeDefinition DataType = Picklist requires AttributePicklistValue records
evals.prompt: I created a Picklist-type AttributeDefinition but the dropdown in the configurator is empty. What did I miss?
evals.notes: Must identify the missing AttributePicklistValue records as the cause.

evals.description: Amendment vs edit-in-place — original transaction is immutable post-activation
evals.prompt: A customer wants to add 10 seats to their active subscription. Can I just update the Quantity on the original TransactionLineItem?
evals.notes: Must clearly state that activated TLIs are immutable and that an amendment transaction must be created instead.

evals.description: RevenueTransaction ownership — RevRec engine only, no manual creation
evals.prompt: Can I insert RevenueTransaction records manually via Apex to backfill revenue recognition for migrated data?
evals.notes: Must advise against manual RevenueTransaction creation. Must state the RevRec engine owns the object lifecycle.

evals.description: PricebookEntry still required in new-core Revenue Cloud
evals.prompt: I'm using PriceAdjustmentSchedule to drive all my pricing. Do I still need to create PricebookEntry records for my products?
evals.notes: Must confirm PricebookEntry is still required as the waterfall baseline, even when adjustment schedules drive the net price.

evals.description: ProductClassification reuse risk — version, do not edit in place
evals.prompt: I want to add a new attribute to my ProductClassification. Can I just add it to the existing classification record?
evals.notes: Must warn that editing a live classification retroactively affects all products using it, and recommend versioning instead.

evals.description: Pricing waterfall sequence — multiple schedules applied in Sequence order
evals.prompt: I have two PriceAdjustmentSchedule records on a PricebookEntry. How does the system decide which one to apply first?
evals.notes: Must explain that PriceAdjustmentSchedule.Sequence controls application order, ascending. Must describe stacking behavior.
